channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: NIPOST bets on digital infrastructure beyond traditional mail
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NIPOST is rolling out a national digital postcode system, a critical infrastructure project set to transform location, identity, and access in Nigeria.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nipost-bets-on-digital-infrastructure-beyond-traditional-mail/

item.title: Tinubu orders probe of Meta, Google, AI platforms
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu directs FCCPC to investigate Meta, Google, and AI platforms for anti-competitive practices and content exploitation against Nigerian media
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-orders-probe-of-meta-google-ai-platforms/

item.title: UBA names Nnorom chairman as Elumelu exits board
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
United Bank for Africa (UBA) announces Emmanuel Nnorom as its new chairman, succeeding Tony Elumelu who retires after 12 years. Learn more about UBA’s lead
Read More: https://punchng.com/uba-names-nnorom-chairman-as-elumelu-exits-board/

item.title: Telcos urge states to cut fibre deployment costs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian telcos are urging state governments to reduce fibre deployment costs, warning that high charges and infrastructure damage threaten broadband expan
Read More: https://punchng.com/telcos-urge-states-to-cut-fibre-deployment-costs/

item.title: Between the chief of controversies and prince of fraud
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Explore the controversy surrounding President Tinubu’s Chief of Staff, Femi Gbajabiamila, and allegations of fraud. Uncover Nigeria’s governance issues.
Read More: https://punchng.com/between-the-chief-of-controversies-and-prince-of-fraud/

item.title: Herders’ violence: Enough excuses, implement ranching
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Enough excuses! Herders’ violence is a national security emergency. Learn why implementing ranching is crucial to end open grazing and restore peace in Nig
Read More: https://punchng.com/herders-violence-enough-excuses-implement-ranching/

item.title: Joshua Ibanga: The unending scourge of police brutality
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The tragic death of Joshua Ibanga highlights the unending scourge of police brutality in Nigeria. Learn about the latest incident and calls for justice.
Read More: https://punchng.com/joshua-ibanga-the-unending-scourge-of-police-brutality/

item.title: Minister’s directive delayed NELFUND employees’ resumption – MD, Sawyerr
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NELFUND MD, Akintunde Sawyerr, explains the prolonged delay in staff resumption, attributing it to a directive from the Minister of Education. Learn more a
Read More: https://punchng.com/ministers-directive-delayed-nelfund-employees-resumption-md-sawyerr/

item.title: UNILAG Arts Faculty opens N30m clinic
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The UNILAG Faculty of Arts, in partnership with alumni and Cityview Hospital, has launched a N30m clinic to provide accessible healthcare for students and
Read More: https://punchng.com/unilag-arts-faculty-opens-n30m-clinic/

item.title: FAAN, three states owe NAMA N25bn amid funding concerns
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
FAAN and three state governments owe the Nigerian Airspace Management Agency (NAMA) over N25bn, raising concerns about NAMA’s operations and funding.
Read More: https://punchng.com/faan-three-states-owe-nama-n25bn-amid-funding-concerns/

item.title: NOC urges Nigerians to embrace exercise for healthy living
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The President of the Nigeria Olympic Committee (NOC), Engr. Habu Gumel, has urged Nigerians to embrace regular physical exercise as a vital step towards healthy living. The post NOC urges Nigerians to embrace exercise for healthy living appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Kwara APC Governorship Aspirants Protest Against AbdulRazaq
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The lingering crisis in the Kwara State chapter of the All Progressives Congress (APC) deepened on Monday as 10 governorship aspirants and other key stakeholders staged a solidarity rally in Ilorin, protesting what they described as Governor AbdulRahman AbdulRazaq’s exclusionary

item.title: Oshiomhole and the politics of language
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Words are not innocent. They carry weight. They carry memory. They carry consequences. A careless word can ignite a riot. A reckless phrase can destroy a reputation. A misleading sentence can alter the course of public opinion. A nation may survive bad roads, poor electricity, and even economic hard

item.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's

item.title: Jailers and officials at Russia's 'torture prisons' in Ukraine exposed by BBC
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Former prisoners accuse the men of abuse in detention centres and want to see them brought to trial.

item.title: Ronaldo's World Cup career ends in tears as 'pathetic' Martinez departs
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: BBC Sport looks at the end of Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career as he fails to win football's biggest prize.

item.title: Imo: 8 kidnapped Scripture Union delegates freed after 22 days
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Eight members of the Scripture Union (SU) abducted along the interstate boundary between Imo and Anambra on June 14, while returning from a national conference, have regained their freedom. The post Imo: 8 kidnapped Scripture Union delegates freed after 22 days appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Abia, Quantus Foundation inaugurate patient liaison officers at hospitals to promote excellence
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Abia State Government, in conjunction with Quantus Medical Foundation, a United States-based non governmental organisation (NGO) committed to excellence in healthcare, has inaugurated Patient Liaison Officers (PLOs) in Aba General Hospital, bringing the number of such inauguration to three in th

item.title: Abia oil communities commend Tinubu, others over Reps seat allocation
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The people of Asa in Ukwa West Local Government Area of Abia State have lauded President Bola Tinubu, Governor of Imo State, Hope Uzodimma, and the National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Professor Nentawe Yilwatda, for giving ticket to Sam Nwogu from the area for the Ukwa House o

item.title: Enugu West PDP stakeholders reject Amadi’s nomination, insist on state chairman’s return
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Stakeholders of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) in Enugu West Senatorial Zone have rejected the purported nomination of Hon. Dennis Amadi as the party’s senatorial candidate, insisting that no valid primary election was conducted in the zone to produce him. The post Enugu West PDP stakeholders re

item.title: Israeli parliament backs first step towards October 7 inquiry
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Israel's parliament approved the first reading of a bill on Monday to establish a commission of inquiry into the security failings surrounding Hamas's October 7, 2023 attack, though opposition lawmakers boycotted the vote, arguing the proposed body would lack independence.

item.title: Ronaldo says his last World Cup is over but Portugal decision can wait
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Portugal captain admits his team's last-16 match against Spain was his final outing in the World Cup.

item.title: Anambra presents SUVs to 10 judges
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: In a move aimed at improving the welfare and mobility of judicial officers, the Anambra State Government has presented brand-new Sport Utility Vehicles (SUVs) to ten judges, ensuring they can conveniently commute to their offices and discharge their duties efficiently. The post Anambra presents SUVs

item.description: Peter Uzoho  A top official of one of the International Oil Companies (IOCs) operating in Nigeria has called for urgent conversion of recent oil and gas Executive Orders to legislation,

item.title: Experts call for scalable solutions to meet rising housing demand

item.description: Disturbed by the current housing crisis with galloping affordability gap affecting mostly low income earners, emerging real estate market industry experts under the auspices of Association of Housing Corporations of Nigeria (AHCN), are calling for urgent scalable solutions to meet the rising housing

item.title: Ebonyi NUJ decries delay in distribution of farm inputs
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigeria Union of Journalists (NUJ), Ebonyi State Council, has condemned in strong terms the recurrent delay in the distribution of government farm inputs to farmers. The post Ebonyi NUJ decries delay in distribution of farm inputs appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Cross River NDC Unveils Five Strategies Ahead of 2027 Polls
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Cross River State chapter of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, has unveiled five strategic measures to reposition the party ahead of the 2027 general elections. The decisions were reached during a State Working Committee meeting held at the party secretariat in Calabar. A statement issued by

item.title: FG gives South East smallholder farmers 80,640 fertiliser bags
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has distributed 80,640 bags of fertiliser to 20,160 registered smallholder farmers across Imo, Enugu, Anambra and Ebonyi states. The post FG gives South East smallholder farmers 80,640 fertiliser bags appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.description: Stories by Steve Aya Nigeria’s Chief Justice, Justice Kudirat Kekere-Ekun, has unveiled a major digital transformation agenda for the nation’s Judiciary, declaring that while artificial intelligence is revolutionising legal practice

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The necessity of value addition to raw materials and commodity products before exporting through domestic processing is gaining attention as Nigeria’s pathway to industrialisation and economic prosperity, writes Dike Onwuamaeze

item.title: State Police: A Constitutional Imperative and the Question of Federal Intervention
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Nigeria stands at a constitutional turning point. With the Senate having passed the State Police Bill, the long debate over the decentralisation of policing is at last, close to law.

item.title: CMSA Summit: Experts Want Stronger Capital Market Reforms
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Stakeholders in Nigeria’s capital market have called for stronger institutional reforms, improved regulatory coordination and enhanced investor protection to sustain market growth and strengthen confidence in the country’s financial system.

item.description: Leading commercial law firm, Babalakin & Co., has reaffirmed its commitment to strengthening Nigeria’s creative economy by rewarding outstanding young artists and promoting the role of law in protecting creativity,

item.description: The vice-presidential candidate of the National Democratic Congress, Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso, has commiserated with former Bauchi State Deputy Governor, Baba Tela, over the death of his two children, Mustapha and Ummi, in a fatal road accident. Kwankwaso conveyed his condolences in a statement shared o

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Save the Children says families in and around the city of el-Obeid are facing increasingly difficult conditions.

item.title: Trump calls Walmart ‘patriotic’ for cutting beef prices — in a stark change of tune
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The president hasn’t always approved of Walmart’s moves, having criticized the chain’s response to tariffs last year.

item.title: A conman amidst conmen
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: There is something extremely irritating about the current national umbrage at Mr. Adeniyi Adeyemi and his now discredited Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council/Presidential Economic Advisory Council (PFIPC). The umbrage, to all intents and purposes, is largely feigned. The post A conma

item.title: First Lady Harvests Vegetables From Her Garden At State House
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Nigeria’s First Lady, Oluremi Tinubu, on Monday harvested vegetables from her garden at the State House in Abuja, using the occasion to encourage Nigerians to embrace and sustain home gardening as a means of enhancing household food security. Senator Tinubu said home gardening remains a practi

item.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

item.title: Livestock Ministry unveils digital platform, pushes value addition in leather industry
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Federal Government has reaffirmed its commitment to transforming Nigeria’s livestock sector through digital innovation and strategic investments aimed at promoting industrialisation, value addition and private sector-led growth across the livestock value chain. The post Livestock Ministry unveil

item.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Look to Fed for Interest-Rate Outlook
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Gold was little changed as traders awaited minutes from the Federal Reserve’s last meeting for fresh insights into the path for US interest rates.

item.title: Sagna tips France for World Cup glory, says Africa closing gap on football’s elite
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Beyond France's prospects, Sagna said African football has made remarkable progress, arguing that the gap between the continent and Europe's traditional powers continues to narrow due to improved organisation, youth development and stronger football structures.

item.title: Spain's World Cup challenge built on historic defence
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: If Spain are to get their hands on the World Cup trophy again, they may have their defence, rather than their attack, to thank.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Rosemary Iwunze PRIVATE healthcare providers are expanding investments in specialist medical services as rising cases of chronic diseases, an ageing population and increasing demand for quality healthcare continue to reshape Nigeria’s healthcare landscape. The latest move came from OneHealt

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.description: Samsung Electronics Co.’s quarterly profit surged 19-fold, just surpassing elevated expectations due to relentless demand for memory chips needed in AI data centers.

item.title: Russian missile and drone strikes kill 24 in Kyiv ahead of NATO summit
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Russia launched a deadly barrage of missiles and drones on Kyiv on Monday, killing at least 24 people and wounding more than 100 in its second major strike on the capital in a week. The attack came on the eve of a NATO summit, where President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to press allies for strong

item.title: The Nasdaq-100 has been far more volatile than the S&P 500. Now add SpaceX to the mix.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: While SpaceX is due to join the Nasdaq-100 on Tuesday, it won’t become part of the S&P 500 for at least another year — likely furthering the volatility spread between those two indexes.

item.title: Shettima, Okonjo-Iweala, Brazilian, Chinese Investors For Delta Summit
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …State Targets Agriculture, Power, Blue Economy, Solid Minerals The Delta State Government on Monday said 25 prospective investors from Brazil and China have indicated interest in the forthcoming Delta State Investment and Economic Summit, as Vice President Kashim Shettima and Director-General of t

item.title: Spain end Cristiano Ronaldo’s World Cup dream with late win over Portugal
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo’s FIFA World Cup career came to a heartbreaking end as Spain edged Portugal 1-0 in the Round of read more Spain end Cristiano Ronaldo’s World Cup dream with late win over Portugal

item.title: FEPPPAN Hails Tinubu Over N39bn Pension Debt Clearance
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal Parastatals and Private Sector Pensioners Association of Nigeria (FEPPPAN) has commended President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for approving the liquidation of more than N39 billion in inherited Defined Benefit Scheme (DBS) pension liabilities. The pensioners’ union, in a statement jointly s

item.title: Full Writeup of the Windows GDID
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/SmtimesIWndr/gdid-reversal Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48811081 Points: 23 # Comments: 8

item.title: Oil Holds Drop as Saudi Arabian Price Cut Amplifies Glut Concern
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil held a drop on signs of growing oversupply, with Saudi Arabia slashing prices and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picking up.

item.title: Reps Pass 363 Bills In 3 Years, Introduced 2,747 Bills
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …1782 Awaiting Second Reading The House of Representatives has passed a total of 363 bills as of June 15, 2026, marking three years of its four-year legislative tenure. The Chairman of the Committee on Rules and Business, Hon. Francis Waive (APC, Edo), disclosed this on Monday while giving a breakd

item.title: I’m not worried about Obi, northerners will never vote for him – Ali Modu Sheriff
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: When reminded that Obi won substantial votes in states including Plateau and Nasarawa during the last presidential election, Sheriff maintained that circumstances had since changed. The post I’m not worried about Obi, northerners will never vote for him – Ali Modu Sheriff appeared first
